# Karl Wiegers, *Software Requirements* - Cite-Only Pointer

This document is a cite-only pointer. It names the Karl Wiegers acceptance-criteria and requirements-quality guidance the BRD Builder workflow references, links to the upstream publisher, and does not redistribute the book text or any template prose from Process Impact.

## What This Source Is

Karl Wiegers and Joy Beatty's *Software Requirements* (3rd edition, Microsoft Press, 2013) is a widely cited industry reference on business and software requirements engineering. The companion site, Process Impact, publishes a set of Microsoft Word templates for requirements artifacts, including acceptance-criteria and use-case templates.

The BRD Builder references this source by name in two ways:

* As the canonical industry treatment of acceptance-criteria authoring rules and requirements-quality attributes that informed the original Microsoft atomicity checklist in [atomicity-checklist.md](atomicity-checklist.md).
* As an authoritative external reading the workflow can point a stakeholder at when they ask for a deeper treatment of acceptance-criteria practice than the BRD Builder embeds.

## Why Cite-Only

*Software Requirements* is a commercial Microsoft Press publication. Process Impact templates are distributed under a license that grants in-organization use but does not permit redistribution in an open-source agent collection. Embedding the book text, the template tables, or Wiegers' definition prose is therefore out of scope for this repository.

The BRD Builder's posture is:

* Reference Karl Wiegers' *Software Requirements* by name and edition when explaining the lineage of an acceptance-criteria rule or quality attribute.
* Do not paraphrase Wiegers' prose. Definitions in this repository are written from scratch as original Microsoft content.
* Do not embed Process Impact templates. The canonical acceptance-criteria pattern the BRD Builder uses is documented in [given-when-then.md](given-when-then.md), and the canonical checklist is documented in [atomicity-checklist.md](atomicity-checklist.md); both are original Microsoft content under CC BY 4.0.

## Upstream Source

[https://www.wiegers.net/](https://www.wiegers.net/) (accessed 2026-05-25) - Karl Wiegers' author and Process Impact site, where the book's publication details, the template license terms, and current revisions are obtained.

## License

This pointer file is original Microsoft content licensed under [CC BY 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/). *Software Requirements* (Karl Wiegers and Joy Beatty, 3rd ed., Microsoft Press, 2013) and Process Impact templates are the property of Karl Wiegers / Process Impact and are subject to their own licenses at the upstream source.

## Citations

* Karl Wiegers and Joy Beatty, *Software Requirements*, 3rd edition, Microsoft Press, 2013.
